title: N005: Add CI integration and OCI release workflow
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'Helm Chart Packaging

Chart structure under charts/'

Add Helm chart CI validation and OCI release automation. CI job in .github/workflows/ci.yml: runs helm lint, ct lint (chart-testing), and helm-unittest on PRs touching charts/. New .github/workflows/helm-release.yml: triggered on chart/v* tags, logs into ghcr.io, runs helm package, then helm push to oci://ghcr.io/<org>/charts/memcached-operator. Chart version in Chart.yaml must match the tag. Includes chart README with install instructions (helm install from OCI registry), upgrade instructions, and CRD upgrade guidance for both crds/ and managedByHelm strategies.

**Rationale:** CI prevents broken charts from merging. The OCI release workflow enables users to install the chart directly from GHCR without manual packaging. The chart/v* tag pattern decouples chart releases from operator image releases, allowing independent chart-only fixes. The README is the primary user-facing documentation for chart consumers.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/helm-release.yml
- charts/memcached-operator/README.md
- charts/memcached-operator/Chart.yaml

stories.title: Chart contributor gets CI feedback on PRs touching charts/
stories.want: CI to automatically run helm lint, ct lint, and helm-unittest when I open or update a PR that modifies files under charts/
stories.so_that: broken chart changes are caught before merge and I get fast feedback on template errors
criteria: A new 'helm-lint' job appears in .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on PRs touching charts/**
The job runs 'helm lint charts/memcached-operator' and fails the PR if lint errors exist
The job runs 'ct lint --charts charts/memcached-operator' (with ct.yaml config) and fails on schema/metadata issues
The job runs 'helm unittest charts/memcached-operator' and fails if any test case fails
The job does NOT run when no files under charts/ are modified (path filter)

stories.title: Release engineer publishes chart to GHCR via tag
stories.want: pushing a chart/v* tag to trigger an automated workflow that packages the chart and pushes it to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator
stories.so_that: chart consumers can install directly from the OCI registry without manual packaging steps
criteria: A new .github/workflows/helm-release.yml workflow triggers on tags matching 'chart/v*'
The workflow logs into ghcr.io using GITHUB_TOKEN
The workflow runs 'helm package charts/memcached-operator'
The workflow runs 'helm push' to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator
The workflow validates that Chart.yaml version matches the tag (e.g. chart/v0.1.0 requires version: 0.1.0)

stories.title: Chart consumer installs from OCI registry
stories.want: clear README instructions for installing the chart from the GHCR OCI registry
stories.so_that: I can deploy the operator using helm install without cloning the repository
criteria: charts/memcached-operator/README.md contains helm install command using oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator
README includes prerequisite section (Kubernetes version, cert-manager, Prometheus CRDs)
README shows how to override common values (image.tag, replicaCount, webhook.enabled)
README includes helm pull command for downloading the chart locally

stories.title: Chart consumer upgrades operator via Helm
stories.want: clear README instructions for upgrading the chart and handling CRD updates
stories.so_that: I can safely upgrade without losing CRDs or Memcached custom resources
criteria: README documents the default CRD behavior (crds/ directory: install-only, not upgraded)
README documents the Helm-managed CRD strategy (crds.managedByHelm=true)
README provides manual CRD upgrade command for the default strategy
README includes pre-upgrade backup instructions for Memcached CRs

stories.title: Release engineer gets clear error when tag/version mismatch
stories.want: the release workflow to fail with a clear error message if the Chart.yaml version does not match the chart/v* tag
stories.so_that: I catch versioning mistakes before a mismatched chart is published to the registry
criteria: If chart/v0.2.0 is pushed but Chart.yaml has version: 0.1.0, the workflow fails
The error message clearly states expected vs actual version
The workflow does NOT push a chart when the version mismatch is detected

stories.title: CI does not regress existing Go workflow jobs
stories.want: the new helm-lint job to be additive to the existing CI workflow without modifying existing jobs
stories.so_that: Go lint, test, build, validate-manifests, and e2e jobs continue to function identically
criteria: Existing lint, test, build, build-push, merge-manifest, validate-manifests, and e2e jobs are unchanged
The new helm-lint job runs independently (no dependencies on other jobs)
The concurrency group and top-level permissions remain unchanged

requirements.description: The CI workflow SHALL include a 'helm-lint' job that runs helm lint, ct lint, and helm-unittest on PRs touching charts/
requirements.rationale: Prevents broken chart templates from being merged; the three tools cover different aspects: helm lint checks template rendering, ct lint validates Chart.yaml schema/versioning, helm-unittest runs declarative test cases

scenarios.name: PR modifying charts/ triggers helm-lint job
scenarios.when: a PR is opened or updated with changes under charts/**
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job runs all three validation steps (helm lint, ct lint, helm unittest)
and_then: job status is reported on the PR checks

scenarios.name: PR not touching charts/ skips helm-lint job
scenarios.when: a PR is opened with changes only outside charts/ (e.g. internal/controller/)
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job is skipped entirely
and_then: no Helm tooling is installed or invoked

scenarios.name: helm lint failure blocks merge
scenarios.when: a PR introduces a template syntax error in charts/memcached-operator/templates/
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job fails at the helm lint step
and_then: subsequent steps (ct lint, unittest) do not run

requirements.description: The CI helm-lint job SHALL use path filtering to only run on changes under charts/
requirements.rationale: Avoids wasting CI minutes on PRs that do not touch chart files; uses dorny/paths-filter or native paths filter in the job condition

scenarios.name: Changes only in charts/ triggers job
scenarios.when: a PR modifies only charts/memcached-operator/values.yaml
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job executes

scenarios.name: Changes only in Go code skips job
scenarios.when: a PR modifies only internal/controller/memcached_controller.go
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job is skipped

scenarios.name: Mixed changes triggers job
scenarios.when: a PR modifies both internal/ and charts/
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job executes because charts/ was touched

requirements.description: A ct.yaml configuration file SHALL be created to configure chart-testing for the project
requirements.rationale: chart-testing (ct) requires a configuration file to know where charts live and how to validate them

scenarios.name: ct.yaml points to correct chart directory
scenarios.when: ct lint is invoked with the ct.yaml config
scenarios.then: it discovers and validates charts/memcached-operator

scenarios.name: ct.yaml validates chart schema
scenarios.when: Chart.yaml is missing a required field
scenarios.then: ct lint reports the schema violation

scenarios.name: ct.yaml sets target-branch to main
scenarios.when: ct lint runs in CI against a PR
scenarios.then: it compares chart changes against the main branch

requirements.description: A new .github/workflows/helm-release.yml SHALL package and push the chart to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator on chart/v* tags
requirements.rationale: Automates chart distribution to GHCR; the chart/v* tag pattern decouples chart releases from operator image releases

scenarios.name: Tag chart/v0.1.0 triggers release
scenarios.when: the tag chart/v0.1.0 is pushed
scenarios.then: the workflow logs into ghcr.io, runs helm package, and pushes to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator

scenarios.name: Non-chart tags do not trigger release
scenarios.when: the tag v0.2.0 (operator release) is pushed
scenarios.then: the helm-release workflow does NOT run
and_then: only the existing release.yml workflow runs

scenarios.name: Workflow uses GITHUB_TOKEN for GHCR auth
scenarios.when: the workflow runs
scenarios.then: it authenticates to ghcr.io using github.actor and secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN
and_then: no additional secrets need to be configured

requirements.description: The helm-release workflow SHALL validate that Chart.yaml version matches the tag before pushing
requirements.rationale: Prevents publishing a chart with a version that does not match the Git tag

scenarios.name: Matching version passes validation
scenarios.when: tag chart/v0.2.0 is pushed and Chart.yaml has version: 0.2.0
scenarios.then: the version validation step passes
and_then: the workflow proceeds to helm package and push

scenarios.name: Mismatched version fails validation
scenarios.when: tag chart/v0.2.0 is pushed but Chart.yaml has version: 0.1.0
scenarios.then: the version validation step fails with message: 'Chart version 0.1.0 does not match tag version 0.2.0'
and_then: the workflow stops before running helm package

scenarios.name: Tag parsing extracts version correctly
scenarios.when: the tag is chart/v1.2.3
scenarios.then: the extracted version is 1.2.3 (chart/v prefix stripped)

requirements.description: The helm-release workflow SHALL run helm lint and helm-unittest before packaging
requirements.rationale: Release-time validation prevents publishing a broken chart even if CI was bypassed

scenarios.name: Lint passes before package
scenarios.when: the release workflow runs
scenarios.then: helm lint charts/memcached-operator runs successfully before helm package

scenarios.name: Lint failure prevents release
scenarios.when: the chart at the tagged commit has a lint error
scenarios.then: the workflow fails at the lint step and does not push to GHCR

scenarios.name: Unit tests run before package
scenarios.when: the release workflow runs
scenarios.then: helm unittest charts/memcached-operator runs before helm package

requirements.description: charts/memcached-operator/README.md SHALL document install, upgrade, and CRD management instructions for OCI registry consumers
requirements.rationale: The chart README is the primary user-facing documentation for chart consumers

scenarios.name: Install instructions use OCI registry
scenarios.when: a user reads the README install section
scenarios.then: the instructions show 'helm install memcached-operator oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator --version <version>'
and_then: a namespace flag example is shown

scenarios.name: Upgrade instructions cover CRD strategies
scenarios.when: a user reads the README upgrade section
scenarios.then: both crds/ directory (default) and crds.managedByHelm strategies are documented
and_then: manual CRD apply command is shown for the default strategy

scenarios.name: README includes values reference
scenarios.when: a user reads the README configuration section
scenarios.then: key values.yaml parameters are documented in a table (image, replicaCount, webhook.enabled, etc.)

requirements.description: The existing CI jobs (lint, test, build, validate-manifests, e2e) SHALL NOT be modified
requirements.rationale: The new helm-lint job is additive; modifying existing jobs risks breaking the Go development workflow

scenarios.name: Existing jobs remain unchanged
scenarios.when: the ci.yml file is updated
scenarios.then: all existing job definitions (lint, test, build, build-push, merge-manifest, validate-manifests, e2e) are byte-identical to before

scenarios.name: Top-level workflow triggers unchanged
scenarios.when: the ci.yml file is updated
scenarios.then: the on.push and on.pull_request triggers remain on branches: [main]

scenarios.name: Concurrency and permissions unchanged
scenarios.when: the ci.yml file is updated
scenarios.then: the concurrency group and top-level permissions block are unchanged

requirements.description: The helm-release workflow SHOULD use least-privilege permissions
requirements.rationale: Following security best practices, the workflow should only request the minimum permissions needed

scenarios.name: Workflow requests packages:write for GHCR push
scenarios.when: the workflow definition is reviewed
scenarios.then: permissions include packages: write and contents: read
and_then: no other write permissions are requested

scenarios.name: Workflow uses GITHUB_TOKEN not PAT
scenarios.when: the workflow authenticates to GHCR
scenarios.then: it uses the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN secret

scenarios.name: Checkout uses contents: read only
scenarios.when: the workflow checks out code
scenarios.then: the checkout step only requires read access to contents

requirements.description: The CI helm-lint job SHALL also run on pushes to main when charts/ files are changed
requirements.rationale: Direct pushes or merge commits to main should also validate the chart

scenarios.name: Push to main with chart changes triggers helm-lint
scenarios.when: a push to main includes changes under charts/
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job runs

scenarios.name: Push to main without chart changes skips helm-lint
scenarios.when: a push to main only includes Go code changes
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job is skipped

scenarios.name: PR to main with chart changes triggers helm-lint
scenarios.when: a PR targeting main includes changes under charts/
scenarios.then: the helm-lint job runs

tasks.title: Create ct.yaml configuration file for chart-testing (REQ-003)
tasks.description: Create ct.yaml in the repository root with chart-dirs: [charts], target-branch: main, and validate-maintainers: false (since maintainers are in Chart.yaml but may not match GitHub usernames). This file is consumed by both the CI helm-lint job and chart-testing CLI.

tasks.title: Add helm-lint job to .github/workflows/ci.yml with path filtering (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-008, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Append a new 'helm-lint' job to .github/workflows/ci.yml. The job: (1) uses dorny/paths-filter@v3 to detect changes in charts/**, (2) if charts changed: installs Helm via azure/setup-helm@v4, installs helm-unittest plugin, installs chart-testing via helm/chart-testing-action@v2, (3) runs 'helm lint charts/memcached-operator', (4) runs 'ct lint --config ct.yaml --all', (5) runs 'helm unittest charts/memcached-operator'. The job runs on both PRs and pushes to main. All existing jobs MUST remain byte-identical — only the new job is appended.

tasks.title: Create .github/workflows/helm-release.yml with version validation, lint, test, package, and push (REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/helm-release.yml triggered on push tags: ['chart/v*']. Permissions: contents: read, packages: write. Steps: (1) checkout, (2) extract tag version by stripping 'chart/v' prefix, (3) read Chart.yaml version via yq or grep, (4) compare tag vs chart version — fail with clear error if mismatch, (5) install Helm via azure/setup-helm@v4, (6) install helm-unittest plugin and run tests, (7) helm lint charts/memcached-operator, (8) helm package charts/memcached-operator, (9) helm registry login ghcr.io, (10) helm push to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator.

tasks.title: Write charts/memcached-operator/README.md with install instructions from OCI registry (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/README.md with sections: (1) Title and description, (2) Prerequisites (Kubernetes v1.28+, cert-manager, optional Prometheus CRDs), (3) Install from OCI registry ('helm install memcached-operator oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator --version 0.1.0 --namespace memcached-operator-system --create-namespace'), (4) Configuration table with key values.yaml parameters (image.repository, image.tag, replicaCount, webhook.enabled, certmanager.enabled, serviceMonitor.enabled, networkPolicy.enabled, crds.managedByHelm, watchNamespaces, leaderElection.enabled, rbac.create), (5) Upgrade section covering both CRD strategies, (6) Uninstall section, (7) CRD upgrade guidance for both crds/ default and managedByHelm strategies.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/backend/ci-pipeline.md to document the helm-lint job (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Add the new 'helm-lint' job row to the jobs table in docs/reference/backend/ci-pipeline.md. Add a new ### helm-lint section describing the job: path filtering with dorny/paths-filter, helm lint, ct lint with ct.yaml, and helm-unittest. Add a row to the 'Local Reproduction' section showing 'helm lint charts/memcached-operator && ct lint --config ct.yaml --all && helm unittest charts/memcached-operator'.

tasks.title: Create docs/reference/backend/helm-release-workflow.md documenting the OCI release workflow (REQ-004)
tasks.description: Create reference documentation for helm-release.yml following the pattern of the existing docs/reference/backend/release-workflow.md. Sections: Overview (trigger, purpose), Trigger (chart/v* tag pattern), Permissions (least-privilege), Job steps (version validation, lint, test, package, push), OCI registry details (oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator), Creating a chart release (tag and push instructions), Versioning (chart version vs appVersion vs operator version).

tasks.title: Update docs/how-to/installation.md to add Helm chart installation option (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Add a new 'Option C: Helm Chart (recommended)' section to the 'Deploy the Operator' section in docs/how-to/installation.md. Include: helm install command from OCI registry, verify installation steps, link to the chart README for full configuration reference. Keep existing Option A (GitHub Release) and Option B (from source) unchanged.

test_specifications.test_function: helm-lint job path filter — charts/ changes detected
test_specifications.story: Chart contributor gets CI feedback on PRs touching charts/
test_specifications.expected: When a PR modifies charts/memcached-operator/values.yaml, the paths-filter step outputs charts_changed=true and the helm-lint steps execute

test_specifications.test_function: helm-lint job path filter — no charts/ changes
test_specifications.story: CI does not regress existing Go workflow jobs
test_specifications.expected: When a PR only modifies Go files, the paths-filter step outputs charts_changed=false and helm lint/ct lint/unittest steps are skipped

test_specifications.test_function: helm lint step validates chart templates
test_specifications.story: Chart contributor gets CI feedback on PRs touching charts/
test_specifications.expected: helm lint charts/memcached-operator succeeds with all default values and fails on invalid template syntax

test_specifications.test_function: ct lint step validates chart metadata
test_specifications.story: Chart contributor gets CI feedback on PRs touching charts/
test_specifications.expected: ct lint --config ct.yaml --all validates Chart.yaml schema, version format, and maintainers

test_specifications.test_function: helm unittest step runs all test suites
test_specifications.story: Chart contributor gets CI feedback on PRs touching charts/
test_specifications.expected: helm unittest charts/memcached-operator runs all YAML test files under charts/memcached-operator/tests/ and reports pass/fail

test_specifications.story: Release engineer publishes chart to GHCR via tag
test_specifications.expected: When tag is chart/v0.1.0 and Chart.yaml version is 0.1.0, the validation step passes and workflow proceeds

test_specifications.story: Release engineer gets clear error when tag/version mismatch
test_specifications.expected: When tag is chart/v0.2.0 but Chart.yaml version is 0.1.0, the workflow fails with 'Chart version 0.1.0 does not match tag version 0.2.0'

test_specifications.story: Release engineer publishes chart to GHCR via tag
test_specifications.expected: helm push pushes the packaged chart to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator with the correct version tag

test_specifications.test_function: lint and unittest run before package
test_specifications.story: Release engineer publishes chart to GHCR via tag
test_specifications.expected: helm lint and helm unittest steps execute before helm package; if either fails, the workflow stops before push

test_specifications.test_function: workflow only triggers on chart/v* tags
test_specifications.story: Release engineer publishes chart to GHCR via tag
test_specifications.expected: The workflow trigger is 'push: tags: ["chart/v*"]' and does not fire on v* operator release tags

test_specifications.story: Chart consumer installs from OCI registry

test_specifications.test_function: README documents both CRD upgrade strategies
test_specifications.story: Chart consumer upgrades operator via Helm
test_specifications.expected: README contains sections for default crds/ directory behavior and crds.managedByHelm=true option with upgrade commands

review_criteria: The helm-lint job in ci.yml runs helm lint, ct lint, and helm unittest — all three pass on the current chart in the repository
The helm-lint job uses path filtering (dorny/paths-filter or equivalent) and does NOT execute when no charts/ files are changed
All existing CI jobs (lint, test, build, build-push, merge-manifest, validate-manifests, e2e) are byte-identical to the version before this feature
helm-release.yml triggers only on chart/v* tags, validates version match, runs lint+unittest, and pushes to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts/memcached-operator
helm-release.yml fails with a clear error message when Chart.yaml version does not match the tag version
charts/memcached-operator/README.md contains install instructions from OCI registry, upgrade instructions with both CRD strategies, values reference table, and uninstall instructions
ct.yaml is present in the repository root with chart-dirs pointing to charts/
docs/reference/backend/ci-pipeline.md is updated with the helm-lint job description
docs/reference/backend/helm-release-workflow.md exists and follows the pattern of existing reference docs
docs/how-to/installation.md includes Helm chart installation as an option
All workflows use least-privilege permissions (packages: write only where needed, contents: read by default)
YAML syntax of all workflow files is valid (can be validated with yamllint)
implementation_notes: Architecture: This feature adds two CI/CD artifacts (helm-lint job + helm-release workflow) and documentation. No Go code changes. The helm-lint job in ci.yml uses dorny/paths-filter@v3 for path-based conditional execution since GitHub's native paths filter on the workflow level would skip ALL jobs, not just helm-lint. ct.yaml at the repository root configures chart-testing (ct) CLI to find charts under charts/ and compare against the main branch. The helm-release.yml workflow uses azure/setup-helm@v4 for Helm CLI, extracts the version from the tag by stripping 'chart/v' prefix, and compares it against the version field in Chart.yaml. helm push uses the OCI protocol natively supported since Helm 3.8+. The .helmignore already excludes tests/ and .github/ from packaged charts. The chart README is the primary user-facing doc; the existing docs/how-to/installation.md and docs/reference/backend/ci-pipeline.md are updated to cross-reference the chart. Key patterns to follow: existing ci.yml structure (permissions per-job, action version pinning), existing docs/reference/backend/ naming convention for reference docs, existing docs/how-to/ for user guides. Risks: (1) dorny/paths-filter may need fetch-depth: 0 on push events to detect changes; (2) ct lint may require a git history to detect changed charts on push events — using '--all' flag bypasses this for the lint job.
